summary:
Pelvic organ prolapse, especially rectocele involving the posterior vaginal wall, is clinically linked to defecatory dysfunction such as strain, incomplete evacuation, and fecal incontinence due to anatomical interference with normal bowel function. This association is well-documented across multiple studies and routine clinical practice. \boxed{A}
